channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Meet the Trump-picked lawmakers giving Speaker Johnson a full House GOP conference
item.description: Newly minted U.S. representatives Jimmy Patronis and Randy Fine spoke with Fox News Digital about their new careers in Congress after they won special elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senators back crackdown on foreign students
item.description: Republican senators are brushing off concerns that high-profile arrests and deportations of foreign students may ostracize a group that is a major contributor to the U.S. economy. International students injected $43.8 billion into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Foreign journalists at US-backed media fear being sent to repressive homelands after Trump's cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After hiding in Thailand for seven years, two Cambodian journalists arrived in the United States last year on work visas, aiming to keep providing people in their Southeast Asian homeland with objective, factual news through Radio Free Asia.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Things to know about the US Mine Safety and Health Administration and the coal industry
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — The U.S. Mine Safety and Health Administration is among the federal agencies selected for spending cuts by the Department of Government Efficiency. Nearly three dozen MSHA offices would have their leases terminated if the plans come to fruition.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY and LEAH WILLINGHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sec Hegseth to visit Panama after Trump's demands for canal's return
item.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Panama next week for meetings with the country's leaders, following remarks from President Donald Trump about potentially acquiring the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China hit brakes on TikTok deal after Trump announced wide-ranging tariffs, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he is signing an executive order to keep TikTok running in the U.S. for another 75 days to give his administration more time to broker a deal to bring the social media platform under American ownership.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: School board leader: It's too early to roll back universal free meals program
item.description: A bill moving its way through the Minnesota Legislature seeks to adjust the popular but expensive universal school meals program passed in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Trump abruptly fires the 4-star general who headed the National Security Agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has abruptly fired the director of the National Security Agency, according to U.S. officials and members of Congress, but the White House and the Pentagon have provided no reasons for the move.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate over parental consent for minors' healthcare
item.description: A parental rights bill moving through the state legislature is sparking heated debate over access to reproductive healthcare for minors and whether or not parents should have a say.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington and Oregon sue over Trump elections order, saying mail voters could be disenfranchised
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Washington and Oregon on Friday became the latest states to ask a court to reject President Donald Trump’s efforts to overhaul elections in the U.S., a day after Democratic officials in 19 others filed a similar lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of law firms, former judges sign onto briefs in support of Perkins Coie
item.description: The brief asks U.S. District Judge Beryl Howell to decide the case in the law firm’s favor and indefinitely block Trump’s order.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump's latest tariffs could affect your wallet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump has unveiled his latest tariffs, and they could have significant implications for your wallet. Trump's sweeping new tariffs, on top of previous levies and retaliation worldwide, are expected to increase prices for everyday items.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and CORA LEWIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans are debating a higher tax rate for millionaires: Report
item.description: Republicans are reportedly considering raising taxes on millionaires to help offset the cost of their tax bill.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from dismantling agency that funds community groups in Latin American countries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed on Friday to block the Trump administration from dismantling an independent agency that distributes grant money to community development groups in Latin American and Caribbean countries. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ asks for pause of order for man in El Salvador to be returned to US
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia is among the Salvadoran migrants who have been deported due to suspected gang affiliations.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: The Energy Department identifies thousands of nonessential positions at risk of DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Energy Department has identified thousands of federal workers it deems "nonessential” and would not be protected if there is another round of large-scale firings, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bernie Sanders, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to visit Bakersfield as part of 'Fighting Oligarchy' tour
item.description: Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders and New York Rep. Alexandria Ocasio Cortez are scheduled to make an appearance in Bakersfield as part of their "Fighting Oligarchy" tour.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Budget begins to take shape as leaders huddle
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst talks with the top budget adviser to the governor: Erin Campbell, commissioner of Minnesota Management and Budget. Then, a conversation with the new DFL Party Chair Richard Carlbom.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna clashes with GOP House leaders, plans tele-town hall
item.description: The Pinellas County Republican is pushing to allow new parents the ability to vote by proxy, or remotely, for up to 12 weeks after having a baby.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Legislation aimed at protecting Florida state parks stuck in committee
item.description: What all started with a leaked proposal to build golf courses and pickleball courts at state parks last year has now resulted in legislation that aims to protect natural grounds from those very acts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
